rationale.completeness: Fully addresses both requirements: caching layer for SEC filings and web content (Brave search, EDGAR, HTML parsing) and JSON custom dataset support in both evaluate.py and run_agent.py.
rationale.correctness: The Cache.set() API has an awkward signature (value first, then *args) that is inconsistent with Cache.get() and error-prone; the singleton get_cache() ignores parameters after first initialization which can silently misconfigure callers; and the memory eviction policy removes entries during set before the new entry is added, meaning the count check is off-by-one.
rationale.style: Follows project conventions reasonably well—uses dataclasses, type hints, logging via get_logger, and env var patterns consistent with the codebase—though the asymmetric set/get signatures and duplicate JSON-parsing logic between run_agent.py and evaluate.py deviate from DRY principles.
rationale.quality: Test coverage is good and the two-tier cache design is sound, but the duplicated JSON-loading logic in run_agent.py should reuse load_json_dataset from evaluate.py, the corrupted-file handler silently swallows the exception variable, and the cache_dir.mkdir call in __init__ runs even when caching is disabled.
